rate_page_title: Rate Limiting, Input Validation & CORS - API Security Reference Hub
rate_subtitle: Explains defensive implementation patterns to protect your API from abuse.
rate_rl_desc: Rate limiting is a fundamental defense mechanism to protect your system from API abuse, DoS attacks, and scraping.
rate_rl_fixed_char: Counts within a fixed time window
rate_rl_slidecnt_char: Weighted average of previous and current windows
rate_rl_leaky_char: Processes requests at a constant rate
rate_rl_comment_global: // Global limit: applied to all APIs
rate_rl_comment_login: // Login attempts limited to 5 per 15 minutes
rate_rl_key_desc: By applying rate limits using composite keys such as API key, user ID, or endpoint in addition to IP address, you can minimize the impact on legitimate users.
rate_iv_desc: All API inputs may contain malicious data. Always validate on the server side.
rate_iv_allowlist_desc: Define a list of permitted values. Safer than a denylist (blocklist). Can handle new attack patterns.
rate_iv_type_desc: Strictly check data types, maximum character counts, and numeric upper/lower bounds. A fundamental defense against buffer overflows.
rate_iv_sanitize_desc: Rejecting invalid input is safer than "fixing" it. Sanitization may produce unexpected transformations.
rate_iv_example_nosql: Ensure input is a string (reject Objects)
rate_cors_desc: CORS is a mechanism that controls the browser's Same-Origin Policy. Misconfiguration can lead to serious security risks.
rate_cors_danger_desc: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true cannot be used together. Restrict wildcard usage to public APIs only.
rate_cors_check1: Do not use a wildcard (*) for Access-Control-Allow-Origin (for APIs requiring authentication)
rate_cors_check2: Access-Control-Allow-Methods should include only the required methods
rate_cors_check3: Access-Control-Allow-Headers should include only the required headers
rate_cors_check5: Restrict exposed response headers with Access-Control-Expose-Headers
rate_cors_check6: Never reflect the request Origin back directly in the response
rate_cors_aceh_value: Only necessary headers such as RateLimit-*
rate_headers_comment_csp: # CSP: Since APIs only return JSON, block all script execution
